Antwortvariable.evidence_anchor: The increase from about 9,000 harvested raccoons in 2000/01 to about 71,000 in 2011/12 reflects the extensive amount of suitable habitat for this omnivorous species in Central Europe.

Hauptgruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: As the second largest cause of biodiversity loss worldwide, there is an urgent need to study the dynamics of biological invasions and identify factors limiting the distribution of invasive alien species.

Lebensraum.evidence_anchor: Our focus is (1) to document changes in the distribution and abundance of raccoons, (2) to identify the species-environment relationship and predict which areas will be suitable for future colonization and (3) to apply a dispersal model to predict how fast the raccoon will spread to these areas.

Trend.evidence_anchor: The increase from about 9,000 harvested raccoons in 2000/01 to about 71,000 in 2011/12 reflects the extensive amount of suitable habitat for this omnivorous species in Central Europe.

Untergruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: We analyze national-scale hunting bag data from Germany to predict the dispersal of raccoons in the largest non-native population of the species.
